review_snippets.title: Dr Pepper Reviews 36 - Trustpilot
review_snippets.content:
## On 2 separate occasions- Alabama

On 2 separate occasions- Alabama - Seattle - purchased Diet Dr Pepper - 3 12 pk cans - 3 16 oz bottles. Cans out dated with a nasty taste - bottles Flat. Customer service took all information- assured there would be coupons sent as means of compensation. Both were in 2024 - July - Nov. to date NOTHING- yet I got “rate” the associate- listed my complaint of no receipt- still nothing. What a rip off -

[Comparative toxicity, phytochemistry, and use of 53 Philippine ...]: revealed the presence of tannins in 50 plant extracts, alkaloids in 43, glycosides in 33, flavonoids in 31, steroids in 21, triterpenoids in 20, anthraquinones in 10, and saponins in 8. Extracts from eight plants had LC50 values lower than the potassium dichromate control (approximately 12 μg/mL) and were considered highly toxic; extracts from 21 plants had LC50 values between 12 μg/mL and 100 μg/mL and were considered moderately toxic; extracts from 19 plant extracts, including Vitex negundo and some common vegetables, had LC50 values between 100 μg/mL and 500 μg/mL, and were considered mildly toxic and likely to have reasonable safety margins; five plant extracts, including common vegetables, had LC50 values above 500 μg/mL and were considered essentially nontoxic. [Dr Pepper Reviews 36 - Trustpilot]: ## On 2 separate occasions- Alabama

On 2 separate occasions- Alabama - Seattle - purchased Diet Dr Pepper - 3 12 pk cans - 3 16 oz bottles. Cans out dated with a nasty taste - bottles Flat. Customer service took all information- assured there would be coupons sent as means of compensation. Both were in 2024 - July - Nov. to date NOTHING- yet I got “rate” the associate- listed my complaint of no receipt- still nothing. What a rip off -

Rated 1 out of 5 stars

## I only got 11 cans with soda

I only got 11 cans with soda, one of the cans is empty, it is nit fair that I pay for a 12 pk but get one filled with only air.

### Is this your company?

Claim your profile to access Trustpilot’s free business tools and connect with customers.

### The Trustpilot Experience

#### We're open to all [...] I was the biggest fan of Dr Pepper. Sadly, last week, I purchased a can of the cherry rush limited edition, drank half of it, emptied the rest of the can from the liquid to throw it away (it wasn’t as tasty as the normal Dr Pepper), and as I was about to throw it, I sensed it was still quite heavy for an empty can. I shook it and it was obvious something was in the can, but not liquid. I turned it upside down to try and have a look at what is inside, and I saw a tissue-like thingy in it! Needless to say how disgusted I was, and I had diarrhea for 2 days. The can is still with me and still not wide open as I was trying to reach out to the consumer care dpt, which after a week from the email correspondence and providing them with images and details, they simply sent a letter to say that [...] images and details, they simply sent a letter to say that most probably this is mould that happened in poor storage conditions which they have nothing to do with as they have no control over the storage conditions buyers (shops) store their purchased goods in. I will be sharing a video soon on social media, opening the can and finding out what is really in there. Thank you Dr Pepper, you made me realize how poor and useless your consumer care dpt actually is, and I propose you remove the word ‘care’ from it because you obviously don’t care.
